channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels say 'any Israeli vessel' in nearby Mideast waterways again a target
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels warned shippers early Wednesday that “any Israeli vessel” traveling through nearby Mideast waters is now a target as Israel continues to block aid to the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first nitrogen gas execution. The state says it will appeal
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first death row execution using nitrogen gas, which was scheduled to take place next week. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is known about the deadly January air crash between a passenger jet and US Army helicopter
item.description: Federal aviation officials will continue making sure that planes and helicopters no longer share the same airspace over the nation's capital in the wake of January's midair collision, which killed 67 people and was the deadliest U.S. plane crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: TROY, Mich. (AP) — Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan’s attorney general said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At trial's start, prosecutor blames Iran for plot to assassinate outspoken dissident
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two members of an Eastern European criminal organization were “hired guns for the government of Iran” in a plot to assassinate an Iran-born journalist at her New York City residence three years ago, a prosecutor told a federal jury in an opening statement on Tuesday at the start of a
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blizzard Entertainment president says 'there's a game for you' no matter what type of player you are
item.description: IRVINE, Calif. (AP) — California gaming giant Blizzard Entertainment announced Thursday that its popular event BlizzCon is coming back after several years off. The celebration of all things Blizzard, which will be held at the Anaheim Convention Center, is scheduled for September 2026.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ official says she was dismissed over Mel Gibson's gun rights
item.description: "Decisions are being made based on relationships and loyalty, not based on facts or expertise or sound analysis,” Oyer said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: The boy, Thomas Cooper, was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at Oxford Center, a medical facility in a Detroit suburb, on Jan. 31. His mother was standing next to the chamber and was injured trying to save her boy.
item.dc:creator: Daylyn Huff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NTSB urges ban on some helicopter flights at Reagan airport after deadly collision
item.description: Federal investigators looking for the cause of the January collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people recommended a ban on some helicopter flights Tuesday to improve safety.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How microplastics may be stoking antibiotic resistance
item.description: The microscopic shards of plastic found in every corner of the planet may be exacerbating antibiotic resistance, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works; Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson looks to jam Senate Democrats with bill to avert shutdown
item.description: Before Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) can dare Senate Democrats to reject a Republican-crafted bill to avert an end-of-week shutdown, he has to pull off a feat: keeping his fractious, razor-thin majority united behind the legislation.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Father of missing US student Sudiksha Konanki asks for wider investigation in Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The father of a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki, who went missing in the popular Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana while on spring break with friends, said he has asked authorities to widen their investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get over $25M: What to know about payments
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massive ICE operation nabs nearly 650 illegal aliens in Texas, over 80% with criminal charges or convictions
item.description: ICE executed an operation in Houston that resulted in the arrest of 646 illegal aliens who were convicted of or charged with serious criminal offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge halts effort to deport Columbia protest leader, sets Wednesday hearing
item.description: A federal judge on Monday scheduled a Wednesday court hearing for Mahmoud Khalil, a recent Columbia University graduate who was arrested by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), forbidding the government from moving forward with deportation efforts before then.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico expels alleged US child kidnapper
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – An American who allegedly took his two young children to Mexico without consent and then demanded ransom from the mother is back in Texas to face kidnapping charges.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after 6 children suffer finger lacerations: CPSC
item.description: Consumers who may have purchased or installed the accessories are being instructed to immediately discontinue use.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada slaps electricity tariffs on New York, Minnesota, Michigan
item.description: This surcharge will affect electricity sales for 1.5 million homes and businesses across Michigan, Minnesota and New York,
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4

item.title: Rubio confirms ICE arrest of pro-Palestinian Columbia student protest leader
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has confirmed the arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a green card-holding Columbia University graduate and pro-Palestinian activist, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, KRON4

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI made its way to vineyards. Here's how the technology is helping make your wine
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When artificial intelligence-backed tractors became available to vineyards, Tom Gamble wanted to be an early adopter. He knew there would be a learning curve, but Gamble decided the technology was worth figuring out. The third-generation farmer bought one autonomous tractor.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4
